channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Stanford Professor and the AI-Generated Testimony: A Scandal in the Minnesota Court Case
item.description: A Stanford professor's testimony in a Minnesota court case sparks controversy as plaintiff's lawyers accuse him of using AI to craft his declaration, questioning its reliability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The best cozy games during a stressful Thanksgiving season
item.description: For many, video games are loud, violent, and competitive. But for some they are the opposite: quiet, soft, and low-stakes. Welcome to the world of cozy gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Mr. Miyagi's dramatic return
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to easily record phone calls on your Android
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals an easy trick to record a phone conversation on an Android. Once completed, recordings are easy to find.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The lingering questions around Nvidia’s strong quarterly report
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. Late Wednesday evening, some men were sitting around the Russian baths in Wall Street’s financial district talking about Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Risk and Compliance Exchange 2024: DoD’ Stacy Bostjanick, DCMA’s Jennifer Henderson on finding ‘any means possible’ to help small biz with CMMC
item.description: As the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification program inches closer to reality, DoD prepares defense industrial base for compliance requirements.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI is thinking about building its own browser
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly thinking about developing its own browser with the aim of challenging Google’s dominance in the market, according to The Information. The new browser would have built-in support for Chat GPT and Open AI’s search engine Search GPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From vision to value: A strategic approach to generative AI adoption
item.description: Generative AI has the potential to redefine productivity, create novel applications, and reinvent customer experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Pentagon rolls out new strategy for private 5G
item.description: DoD will rely on existing commercial networks "to the maximum extent possible," but in some cases, will need to run its own 5G infrastructure. The post Pentagon rolls out new strategy for private 5G first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Engineering Protections into Cyber-Physical Systems
item.description: Learn how NIST and NASA are working together to develop a new approach to protecting cyber-physical systems from threats.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: How to bring Android 16’s Notification Cooldown brilliance to any phone today
item.description: Well, I’ll be: We’ve just barely finished welcoming Google’s Android 15 update into the world, and already, Android 16 is teasing us with a tiny early taste. Yes, indeedly: Google has now officially launched the first developer preview of next year’s Android 16 software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS prepares to command an army of AI agents
item.description: According to a recent report from SNS Insider, the global AI agents market was valued at $3.7 billion in 2023. The market is expected to grow to $103.6 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 44.9% during the forecast period from 2024 to 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A vision for the hospital of the future
item.description: In the grand journey of human progress, healthcare is at a tipping point. Much like the decline of civilizations chronicled by Jared Diamond, traditional hospitals, once viewed as bastions of healing, are on the brink of an existential collapse.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Clozel, Fast Company

item.title: Emperor penguin released at sea 20 days after waddling onto Australian beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia has been released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SEC chair to step down on Inauguration Day
item.description: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Gary Gensler will step down from his role when President-elect Trump takes office on Jan. 20, he announced Thursday. “The Securities and Exchange Commission is a remarkable agency,” Gensler said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: SONiC pioneer Aviz Networks raises $17M for AI network management push
item.description: Networking software provider Aviz Networks today announced a $17 million Series A funding round to accelerate its growth in open networking solutions and artificial intelligence capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, ABC 4
